---
title: "Machine learning at the edge"
chapter-id: S08
status: outline
---

::: {.callout-note title="Chapter outline"}
The scope and dependency boundary are stable. Full exposition, worked examples,
exercises, and tested practical evidence still need authoring and review.
:::

## Central question

When can a model run usefully on a microcontroller, and how do you keep its
latency, memory, and energy inside a hard budget?

## Learning outcomes

After completing this chapter, the reader should be able to:

- frame an edge task as a signal-conditioning and inference pipeline with
  explicit accuracy and resource targets;
- estimate the memory, latency, and energy cost of an inference and fit it to a
  microcontroller or accelerator budget;
- apply quantization and feature extraction, and measure the accuracy lost to
  each;
- collect, label, and version representative field data, and defend the
  resulting claim and its failure modes.

## Concept path

- from hand-designed DSP features to learned features;
- model families that fit from kilobytes to a few megabytes;
- quantization, pruning, and operator support;
- fixed-point inference, accelerators, and memory placement;
- on-device measurement of latency, energy, and accuracy;
- data collection, distribution drift, and honest evaluation.

## Evidence and practice

The completed chapter will include a prediction, a worked model, a simulation or
executable check where it adds value, measured or authentic data, a fault or
model mismatch, and a requirements-based design decision. The final exercise set
will mix retrieval, calculation, interpretation, debugging, and open design.

## Connections

Chapter ID: `S08`. Prerequisites and downstream course mappings are maintained in
[`chapters.toml`](../chapters.toml). The [reading roadmap](../roadmap.qmd)
provides reader-facing routes and the practice spine.
